```
app/services/vocabulary_search/
├── __init__.py       # Package exports

title: Population Vocabulary Enrichment: Conditions, Drugs, Procedures
body:
## Overview

Extend vocabulary enrichment beyond Signals (LOINC) to cover Population criteria anchoring.

## Research Summary (2026-01-15)

See full details: `docs/OMOP_VOCABULARY_KNOWLEDGE.md`

### Key Findings
- ATLAS/HELIOS uses raw OMOP - no abbreviation enrichment
- Our LLM enrichment adds unique value (clinical abbreviations)
- **RxNorm hierarchy works well** - Ingredient → all formulations (8K+ descendants per ingredient)
- **SNOMED hierarchy is incomplete** - only 6% of related condition

title: Random truncation
body:
Currently, truncation is performed by keeping the latest events, which may lead to biased exposure to recent data. It can lead to decreased performance if data is filled up by recent but unimportant events. An alternative is to randomly select tokens during truncation.

Proposal:

Implement a random truncation strategy where tokens are sampled randomly instead of simply taking the most recent ones.

Options:

Easy (Static): Perform random truncation once during data preparation.

✅ Simple to imp

title: Add positional encoding to associate lab codes with corresponding values
body:
The attention mechanism in transformers treats all tokens as equally related, without any built-in notion of which tokens are "next to" each other. This makes it difficult for the model to understand which lab value corresponds to which lab code.

Proposal:

Introduce an additional positional or relational encoding to explicitly link each lab value with its corresponding lab code, through a shared embedding.

Suggestion:

Also, allow specifying required background codes (e.g., "gender") via config or enforce presence of at least one "BG_" code.

title: **Title: Exclude Specific Codes from Delayed Censoring**
body:
**Current**

```yaml
concept_pattern_hours_delay:
  "^D": 72
```

**Goal**
Delay all diagnosis codes by 72 h *except* lung-cancer codes (`DC*`), which should be censored immediately.

**Proposed**

```yaml
concept_pattern_hours_delay:
  codes:
    "^D": 72      # 72 h delay for diagnoses
  exclude:
    - "^D/DC.*"   # immediate censoring for lung-cancer codes
```

**Logic**

1. If code matches any `exclude` pattern → delay = 0
2. Else if it matches a `codes` pattern → apply specified delay
3. E

title: Relative min_delta for Early Stopping
body:
Currently, early stopping checks for any improvement over previous epochs. However, when the metric flattens, improvements become marginal. Adding an option for a relative min_delta (e.g., min_delta=0.001 meaning 0.1% improvement) would provide more adaptive stopping criteria.

Proposed Solution:

Allow min_delta to be interpreted as a percentage of the current metric value.
Example: min_delta=0.001 with relative=True stops training if improvement is <0.1%.

Better logging
body:
Use wrapper for logging to separate from the functions. E.g:
```
def log_execution(func):
    @wraps(func)
    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        logger.info(f"Executing {func.__name__} with args={args}, kwargs={kwargs}")
        result = func(*args, **kwargs)
        logger.info(f"Finished {func.__name__} with result={result}")
        return result
    return wrapper
```

title: option_str拼接错误
body:
在generate_fewshot.py中，get_query函数中要求option的选项内容len(v) > 1，对部分题目选项只有1个汉字，会导致拼接的prompt不全；
- 比如591题，"option": {"A": "春", "B": "夏", "C": "秋", "D": "冬", "E": "长夏"}，拼接后只有E选项了；
- 原始函数如下：
```
def get_query(da, use_cot):
    da["option_str"] = "\n".join(
        [f"{k}. {v}" for k, v in da["option"].items() if len(v) > 1]
    )
    if use_cot:
        query = query_prompt_1.format_map(da)
    else:
        query = query_prompt_2.format_map(da)

    return query
```

title: 文档级关系抽取任务定义不一致
body:
尊敬的作者您好，感谢您杰出的工作，您的工作为ChatGPT在信息抽取中的应用做出了很大的贡献！

最近在阅读您代码的过程中我发现您在文档级关系抽取数据集（如DocRED、Re-DocRED、DWIE）等数据集上进行的实验可能与原始的DocRED等数据集的定义不一致。

具体而言，您在文档级关系抽取的prompt中预先给定了可能有关系的实体对，而在DocRED论文定义（[https://aclanthology.org/P19-1074.pdf]）中，我们不能预先给定可能有关系的实体对，而是需要对所有可能的实体对进行分类。所以，在这种情况下您可能造成了一定的数据泄露，导致F1值偏高。举例而言，您在Re-DocRED集上的结果为大约20%-30% F1，远高于最近同类论文报告的约为10% F1结果[https://aclanthology.org/2023.emnlp-main.334.pdf](url)。

请您在论文的更新版本中注明此任务定义的不一致，以避免对社区造成更大的困扰。

问：那你是没有别的语言模型的总结功能吗？

答：作为一种语言模型，我的主要功能是根据我接收到的输入处理和理解自然语言，并提供基于该信息的答案。我没有其他语言模型所拥有的总结功能。
总结涉及将一段较长的文本缩减为较短的版本，而像我这样的语言模型没有能力执行此操作。相反，我能够识别句子结构并理解单词和短语的含义，但我没有能力像人类一样解释或理解整个句子或对话。

title: Speed up generate_embeddings function
body:
Right now the function just iterates over a list of files one at a time and:

1. Converts the image to a tfrecord
2. Calls the CXR foundation API
3. Writes the output to disk

This is highly parallelizable and a simple call to multiprocessing may speed things up a lot.

title: Remove package's tensorflow dependency or loosen version requirements
body:
Removing the dep slims the package down, making it easier to install, and easier for us package maintainers (fewer bugs/conflicts to deal with).

We already decided that TF model code can go purely in the notebooks, and not be part of the package. The current remaining purposes of TF in the package that prevent us from moving the install to the notebooks are:
- Reading tfrecord files
- Generating serialized tfrecords required for the embeddings API

Benefits:
- Pytorch users will be able

title: Update notebooks to use GCS FUSE
body:

App prepares request with PII

Filter PII, replace with tokens

title: Chain LLM Queries to Identify Required HealthKit Data
body:
### Problem

We currently package all the available HealthKit data to the LLM query for a user's request.

### Solution

1. Query the GPT API to ask what health data would be needed to answer the question (a fixed set of types that the user gave permissions to and the needed date range)
2. Query the HealthKit API (with the selected type and date range)
3. Pass the requested data including the question to the GPT API

title: Questions Regarding Instructions #8 and #10
body:
想请教一下作者，关于实践经验的两个问题，还望不吝赐教：
1. 第八条中“对于垂类模型，或许我们更应该关注PT的过程，而不是采集千万百万的SFT数据做训练，我们的建议是大规模预训练+小规模监督微调=超强的LLM模型；”，请问这里的“大规模预训练”是指继承通用大语言模型参数还是在垂类上预训练呢？“小规模监督微调”如何解决通用语言模型无法理解垂域专有词汇或背景知识不足的问题呢（目前是 RAG，但感觉效果还不够好）？如果以医疗chatbot 为例，这里的“千万百万的SFT”指的是人工标记的问答么？

title: refactor: Implement MaxOntSim@K Metric for Benchmark Evaluation
body:
**Description:**
The current Ontology Similarity metric (`OntSim@K`) calculates the *average* of the maximum similarities found for each expected term within the top K results. This doesn't align with the user requirement to capture the single *best* semantic match achieved between *any* expected term and *any* top K retrieved term, similar to how Hit Rate captures the best exact match.

This issue tracks the implementation of a new metric, "Maximum Ontology Similarity @ K" (`MaxOntSim@K`), whic

title: Re-ranking
body:

- Remove error bars from MRR plots as there is no per-case data to calculate proper standard deviations
- Improve plot layout and labeling for better readability
- Add debug logging to display summary file keys for easier diagnostics
- Fix code syntax and formatting issues

This change ensures that MRR plots accurately reflect the values stored in the summary files.

title: Feature: Implement Optional Cross-Encoder Re-ranking
body:
**Motivation:**
Our current RAG system relies on a bi-encoder (Sentence Transformer) for initial dense retrieval (German Query -> English HPO Index). While fast and effective for semantic matching, the ranking precision of the top results can sometimes be suboptimal or show high variance (as observed in benchmarks). Cross-encoders, which process query-document pairs jointly, offer significantly higher accuracy for relevance scoring and are well-suited for improving precision in a re-ranking step

Currently only LocalStack exists for local development. Production deployment requires:

title: 🔒 Security Hardening
body:
## Summary
Harden the application security by removing development defaults, implementing proper secrets management, adding input validation, rate limiting, and request sanitization.

## Background
The current codebase contains **critical security vulnerabilities**:
- Hard-coded development secrets in `utils/config.ts`
- No input validation on API endpoints
- No rate limiting (vulnerable to DoS)
- No request sanitization

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All hard-coded secrets removed from codebase

During fine-tuning with the PFT (Partial Fine-Tuning) mode, the model produces NaN values in predictions, causing training to fail with a `ValueError: Input contains NaN` error in the very first iteration. 1. Set up the environment using COMPASS `requirements.txt`
2. Load the pre-trained model and use `FineTuner.tune_with_test()`

title: The first column must be moved into the index
body:
Why: 

In the section `Reading in ClinVec embeddings into Python` you do `df['node_index'] = df.index` but `df.index` by default takes the values between 0 and length of the array. 

The first column of the CSV stores the actual node indices, so that must be moved into the df index instead.

title: Replace rdkit.six with six
body:
This PR replaces every instance of `rdkit.six` functions with the `six` equivalent. This adds a `six` dependency, but makes TDC compatible with more recent versions of RDKit.

This fixes #374.

As a side note, completely migrating to Python 3 (and removing the `six` dependency) should be trivial; for instance, `pickle` works as a replacement for `six.cPickle` and instances of `six.iteritems(fps)` can be replaced by `fps.items()`.

title: move tests out of the package and use native `pytest` CLI
body:
#### Summary

This PR refactors our test execution strategy by:
- Moving test files out of the main package directory.
- Running tests directly via the `pytest` CLI instead of through a custom Python script.

#### Motivation

This change brings several benefits:

✅ **Cleaner Project Structure**  
Separating test files from the main package helps keep the codebase clean and maintainable. It reinforces a clear distinction between production code and test code.

✅ **Standardized Test E

title: install this package for testing
body:
### Description
This PR removes the additional Conda environment file that was previously used for CI setup. Instead, the CI now installs the package directly, relying on its declared dependencies.

### Why this is beneficial

- **Single Source of Truth**: Ensures that tests run against the same dependencies declared by the package itself.
- **Reduced Maintenance**: No need to maintain a separate environment file in sync with the package dependencies.

title: Add node names instead of node IDs to networkx graph
body:
**Problem:** Each node id (`x_id` and `y_id`) is node type specific. For example, a node ID of "1" for a disease node is different from a node ID of "1" for a gene/protein node. This is (likely) not an issue for training a GNN. However, it can be an issue if users want to do network analysis on PrimeKG because the current `to_nx()` function does not consider node type.

**Solution:** Instead of adding pairs of node IDs as edges, add pairs of node names because they are unique.

title: Fixed a bug and update references in ranged_logAUC
body:
Previously, the ranged_logAUC was calculated firstly by taking the logarithm of the False Positive Rate (FPR), and then taking the intercept of logFPR. This could result in NaN when FPR is 0. The updated version takes the intercept first, which prevents the FPR =0 from happening and then is followed by taking the logarithm. 

The reference is also updated to the lastest manuscript that has detailed explanation of ranged_logAUC.

test cases can be seen in PR #152

title: Tool call in Claude Code crashes when ToolUniverse tool name is > 45 characters long
body:
### Description

The MCP standard specifies that tool names must be maximum 64 characters long [(link)](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/community/seps/986-specify-format-for-tool-names#specification). When the tool name is > 64 characters long, the tool call fails and puts Claude Code in an error loop that requires to exit or clear the session context.

Claude Code tool names are constructed using this template `mcp__{mcp_server_name}__{tool_name}`. Here the prefix is `mcp__tooluniverse__` so th

title: Snmf factorization method does not maintain consistency of sparseness across runs if version='l'
body:
The Snmf factorization method Snmf.factorize() does not handle transposes correctly across multiple runs.
In order to enforce sparseness on the left factor the method transposes self.V (lines 175-176) , fits the model and then back-transposes V and swaps W and H (lines 224-226). However, the initial transpose is done _outside_ run loop which starts in line 178. Therefore, sparseness is _not_ enforced on the same factor across runs, with odd runs being correct and even runs operating on the orign

title: Default kwargs set incorrectly
body: In 539f0692 (see [here](https://github.com/marinkaz/nimfa/commit/539f0692ca81d998e70912c9bcba366a5d2c49ab#diff-1cb9db188ddd1d2bc5f359bc5030a762R189)) `is not None` was introduced to replace implicit boolean use (i.e., `not alpha`) on multiple models, but I believe the meaning was reversed and should be `is None` instead. Currently leaving the defaults as None doesn't set them to a default and leaves them as None, throwing errors on down the line.

title: Scalar operations from `math` often passed to `sop`
body:
Often times scalar-only operations like `math.sqrt` are passed to `sop`.  These are often then applied directly onto a numpy array as in 

```
return op(X + eps, s) if s != None else op(X + eps)
```

Resulting in a type error because `math.sqrt` doesn't know how to operate on numpy arrays. 

It seems like either the calling function needs to pass `np.sqrt` or `sop` needs to be smart enough to select the numpy version if given a scalar version.

title: Calculation of RSS
body:
Current RSS is calculated in the following lines:

``` python
V = self.target(idx)
X = self.residuals(idx = idx)
xX = V - (self.V - dot(self.W, self.H))
return multiply(xX, xX).sum()
```

Note that the function of residuals performs the following:

``` python
return self.V - dot(self.W, self.H)
```

title: SQLite threading issue with CTG client caching
body:
## Problem

The ClinicalTrials.gov (CTG) API client uses `hishel.requests.CacheAdapter()` for HTTP caching, which creates SQLite connections that are thread-local. However, the CTG client uses `asyncio.to_thread()` to run synchronous `requests` calls in separate threads, causing SQLite threading errors:

```
SQLite objects created in a thread can only be used in that same thread. The object was created in thread id 6285668352 and this is thread id 6319321088.
```

## Affected Tools

- `ctg_searc

This rule does not require proof that the voicemail was successfully recorded or the callback was scheduled. In healthcare, this creates compliance and liability risk:

Many healthcare call centers still use basic phone systems without screen-pop capability

title: Daily summaries aggregate by UTC date, not user's local timezone
body:
## Problem

The daily summaries endpoints (`/summaries/activity`, `/summaries/sleep`, `/summaries/body`) aggregate data by UTC date rather than the user's local timezone. This produces incorrect results for users outside UTC.

### Example

A user in US Pacific (UTC-8) takes steps at **11 PM local time on Jan 15**:
- Stored timestamp: `2024-01-16T07:00:00Z` (correct)
- OW aggregates to: **Jan 16** (UTC date)
- User expects: **Jan 15** (their local date)

title: Use native workout types from Apple SDK
body:
Mobile SDK maps workout types before sending to backend. Changing a type on backend requires changes on mobile as well.

Mobile SDK should send native HealthKit types (e.g., `HKWorkoutActivityTypeRunning`) instead of mapped values.

Why? 
- Backend won't need to maintain two mappings (those are currently defined separately in `apple_xml.py` and `apple_sdk.py`)
- Single type change on backend doesn't require mobile SDK update

title: Add ngrok guide for local backend exposure to Developer Guides
body:
Developers testing webhooks (oauth flow, garmin data push) or the Apple Health SDK need to expose their local backend to the internet, but there's currently no documentation on how to do this. This makes it difficult to test OAuth flows, webhook callbacks, and mobile SDK integrations that require public endpoints.

I propose adding `ngrok` as the recommended solution - it requires virtually zero configuration and effort, devs just need to know that it does exist.

Some provider sync implementations have access to calories and distance data from APIs but do not save them to `workout_details` table. These fields appear empty in workout responses.

title: docs: Add 'Data Flow page' to Architecture section
body:
Developers integrating with Open Wearables need a clear understanding of how data flows through the system - from wearable devices to their applications. Currently, the documentation lacks a concise overview that explains the end-to-end journey of health data.

The data flow currently looks like this:
1. Wearable device collects data using its sensors 
2. Wearable app that collects data from the device (BLE)
3. Wearable app syncs with the provider's cloud
4. User authorised a 3rd party developer

title: Docker watch doesn't reload environmental variables
body:
There is two layered issue:

### Pydantic Settings priorities
1. Environmental variables
2. .env file as a source
3. default values from pydantic settings class

### Docker compose watch restarts just process inside container
But entire virtual machine within container is up all the time, so all previous environmental variables stays the same. So even chaning .env file won't change settings in dev mode (because of pydantic priorities).

title: fix(whoop): Add offline scope to enable automatic token refresh
body:
## Description

### Problem
Whoop OAuth tokens expire after 1 hour. Without a refresh token, users must manually re-authorize when tokens expire.

### Root Cause
Whoop only returns a `refresh_token` if the `offline` scope is requested during OAuth authorization.

### Solution
Add `offline` to `WHOOP_DEFAULT_SCOPE` so refresh tokens are returned and stored, enabling automatic token refresh.

### References
- https://developer.whoop.com/docs/developing/oauth/
- https://developer.whoop

title: Add configurable Historical Data Pull Range in settings
body: When a user connects a new device, we currently pull historical data with a hardcoded default range. Developers should be able to configure how far back we fetch data (e.g., 7 days, 30 days, 90 days, or all available) via the settings section in the dev portal. This gives control over initial sync time, storage costs, and API quota usage - especially important for apps that only need recent data or, conversely, need full history for longitudinal analysis.

title: Add deployment documentation
body: We're missing docs on how to deploy the platform. Let's start with a Railway guide since that's what we're using ourselves and the setup is pretty straightforward - basically just creating 4 services, connecting a repo and setting env vars. Later we can expand to other options (Fly.io, self-hosted), but Railway is a good first step to get people up and running quickly.

Developer's backend generates a short-lived token (e.g. valid for 5-10 minutes) for a specific user. The mobile app uses this token once to "sign in" the SDK - the token gets exchanged for an access/refresh token pair, which the SDK stores locally and uses for all further API communication.

title: Preemptively switch to pip for torch-related installations

It may also be worth investigating whether or not alternatives for conda, such as uv or poetry, are worth adopting for package and environment management.

title: Alternative RMSE SE fix to deal with abnormal distirbutions of errors
body: Different, albeit similar in intention, approach that better deals with abnormal distributions of errors and should be more intuitive. This is in contrast to the previous approach using the delta method that was less intuitive because it did not directly measure the variability of errors in their native squared form.

title: Update SE calculation for RMSE to be more robust to large squared errors
body: As the title suggests, this makes the standard error calculation for RMSE more robust to large squared errors. This is done by scaling the the standard error relative to the RMSE, which is less sensitive to possible outliers.

title: Correct SNR calculation to reflect reference
body:
This PR makes some corrections to the SNR calculation in order to properly reflect the approach used in the CHROM paper, and as mentioned in the bottom of page six of [this paper](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7899506/pdf/boe-12-1-494.pdf).

Using the `PURE_UBFC-rPPG_TSCAN_BASIC.yaml` for testing, here's results (including SNR) prior to corrections:

FFT MAE (FFT Label): 1.2974330357142858 +/- 0.3951013616658031
FFT RMSE (FFT Label): 2.8704957923240366 +/- 3.0489809798112115

body:
This PR creates a unified log path for pre-trained models, saved test-time outputs, and Bland-Altman plots. In the near future, it will also include training and validation loss curves, as well as learning rate curves. 

I recommend not paying too much attention to f98534c08ff38ed3a4d716d2dc695517f50e2b69, as that's a very simple change (enabling Bland-Altman plots by default) applied to all of the config files.
